Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First the case ID should be the order_id So each order is a separate process instance I need to track the lifecycle of each order from creation to payment Looking at the tables Orders table has order creation info Order Lines shows when items were picked Delivery has dispatch and arrival dates Invoice has when invoices were issued Payments show when payments were made

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to derive activities for each order The prompt suggests Order Created from Orders table Items Picked events from Order Lines Order Dispatched and Order Arrived from Delivery Invoice Issued from Invoice Payment Received from Payments

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since picking items is a process that might happen sequentially different items at different times it makes sense to have separate events for each item picked Similarly for deliveries if an order has multiple deliveries like order 1002 we need separate Order Dispatched and Order Arrived events for each shipment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait but the invoice issue date is after the delivery arrival That seems odd but possible if invoicing happens after delivery Ill go with the dates as given

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For order 1001 the sequence should be Order Created Items Picked Order Dispatched Order Arrived Invoice Issued Payment Received

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Similarly check order 1002 there are two delivery records two invoices and two payments

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the dispatch date for DLV 5003 is after DLV 5002 dispatch but before DLV 5002 arrival So the sequence is dispatch first shipment dispatch second shipment arrival first shipment arrival second shipment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For resources actors Order Created uses sales person from Orders Items Picked uses picker from Order Lines Order Dispatched and Arrived uses carrier from Delivery Invoice Issued uses billing person from Invoice Payments table does not have a resource so probably leave resource blank for payment events

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Possible assumptions Maybe order 1004 was canceled before delivery so no delivery invoice payment Or data is incomplete

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt says If data is missing or ambiguous document any assumptions made So for order 1004 the event log should only include the events that exist

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for each event I need to assign Case ID order_id Activity Name Timestamp Resource and other attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For Order Created include customer_id customer_type order_priority For Items Picked include item_id quantity item_description item_category For Order Dispatched and Arrived include delivery_id delivery_note For Invoice Issued include invoice_id invoice_amount For Payment Received include payment_id payment_method payment_reference

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now ordering these events by timestamp for order 1001 order 1002 order 1003 order 1004 and order 1005

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To keep the table useful include specific columns for the main attributes and leave irrelevant columns empty

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to make sure all events are included in chronological order per order
